title: How the Other Half Lives: Studies Among the Tenements of New York
author: Riis, Jacob A. (Jacob August)
summary: Jacob A. Riis documents New York’s tenements, exposing overcrowded, unsanitary housing that breeds disease, pauperism, crime, and moral contagion. Drawing on official records, statistics, photographs, and personal investigations, Riis traces the evolution of tenements, describes neighborhoods (Chinatown, Jewtown, the Bend, Hell’s Kitchen), and details lodging-houses, sweatshops, children’s plight, waifs, tramps, and the harvest of social ruin. He indicts speculative landlords, public neglect, and the city’s indifference while urging reform: clean homes, sanitary oversight, and humane policies. The book combines journalism, social analysis, and moral appeal to awaken citizens to a civic responsibility for the Other Half. It demands action immediately.
